mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, compressing term premiums and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 5.5% within a single month, it signals labor market deterioration that historically precedes equity drawdowns as corporate earnings expectations adjust downward.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: The joint behavior of rising housing starts and falling industrial production indicates a sectoral rotation from manufacturing to services, which historically supports broad equity valuations as consumer-facing sectors outperform.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict 60-day forward returns with a directional accuracy exceeding 55% over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-rate environment, CPI readings below 190 indicate disinflationary pressure that allows the central bank to pivot, compressing term premiums and driving positive equity returns over the next quarter.
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with elevated policy rates
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL remains below 190 for consecutive quarters while SPX 60-day forward returns average negative or zero.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total employment reflects robust aggregate demand and wage-driven consumption, which supports corporate earnings and yields positive S&P 500 returns over a 1-week horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward directional accuracy drops below 50% over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When retail spending declines by more than 1.5% month-over-month, it signals a sharp contraction in consumer expenditure that typically leads to negative equity returns over the following month as earnings revisions turn negative.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 65% of instances following the trigger threshold.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous rise in industrial production and stable unemployment indicates a supply-side productivity shock that expands profit margins without triggering wage inflation, driving positive equity returns over a 1-week horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative Rank IC over any 2-year rolling window, or forward returns do not significantly outperform the baseline in the top decile of signal strength.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid policy rate increases, quarterly GDP growth decelerating below 1.5% annualized signals a demand-driven recession risk that historically triggers sharp equity drawdowns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns remain positive in more than 70% of instances following GDP quarterly contractions during rate-hike periods.
